file: How to Handle Difficult Clients as a Freelancer: Tips on Diplomacy & Setting Boundaries for Success
brief: Regular check-ins and updates can help keep your client  informed and involved in the process. Freelancing offers incredible freedom,  but it also comes with its own set of challenges. One of the most daunting is dealing with clients  who are, let's say, less than easygoing. They might be overly demanding, unclear in their communication,  or even late with payments.
sourceTitle: How to Handle Difficult Clients as a Freelancer: Tips on Diplomacy & Setting Boundaries for Success

file: Mastering Client Disputes: A Freelancer's Guide to Conflict Resolution | Gig Economy Disagreements
brief: By addressing disputes strategically,  you can turn potential setbacks into stepping  stones for professional development. By recognizing these pitfalls, you can take proactive steps  to clarify expectations and prevent issues from escalating. Understanding the types of disputes that can arise  is your first step toward maintaining balance and control. First, scope changes occur when a client requests additional work  beyond the initial agreement.
sourceTitle: Mastering Client Disputes: A Freelancer's Guide to Conflict Resolution | Gig Economy Disagreements
